---
title: "Icelandic Fishing Logbooks"
---

Icelandic fishing vessels are required to record their catches and fishing
activity in logbooks. These records — covering gear type, location, timing,
and species caught — form the backbone of fisheries monitoring and stock
assessment in Iceland.

This site documents the work of consolidating logbook data from three separate
source systems into a single, consistent dataset spanning several decades. The
main challenges are that the systems use different formats and codes, their
records partially overlap in time, and the data contain various quality issues
that need to be identified and resolved before the data can be used reliably.

The pages here cover the structure of the raw data, the processing steps
applied to each source, known data quality problems and how they are addressed,
and the logic used to merge the sources into one unified record.
